# pfsense_firewall_nat_npt_rules (Data Source)

Retrieves all [NAT NPt](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/nat/npt.html) rules.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "pfsense_firewall_nat_npt_rules" "all" {}

output "rule_count" {
  value = length(data.pfsense_firewall_nat_npt_rules.all.rules)
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `rules` (List of Object) List of NAT NPt rules. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules))

<a id="nestedatt--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules`

Read-Only:

- `description` (String) Description used as the unique identifier for this NAT NPt rule.
- `destination_not` (Boolean) Invert the destination prefix match.
- `destination_prefix` (String) External global unicast routable IPv6 prefix in CIDR notation (e.g. '2001:db8::/64'). The prefix size must equal the source prefix size. May also be an interface name to use a delegated (track6) prefix.
- `disabled` (Boolean) Disable this NPt rule.
- `interface` (String) Network interface this NPt rule applies to (e.g. 'wan', 'lan', 'opt1'). Typically the WAN.
- `source_not` (Boolean) Invert the source prefix match.
- `source_prefix` (String) Internal (LAN) ULA IPv6 prefix in CIDR notation (e.g. 'fd00:1::/64'). The prefix size is applied to the external prefix.
